downloading a payload (Content-Length pre-check + streaming body).
Custom fetchers are free to ignore the bag entirely; the field
exists so hosts that wrap

limits the format would have applied.

readme.text: ).** The package surface is implemented and tested, but the emitted HTML/CSS contract may evolve before

.

HTML export plugin for Anvilkit Studio. Turns
documents into standalone HTML with emitted CSS, optional asset inlining, and an opt-in Studio header action for interactive export flows when the host supplies a

callback. The reference implementation of the Anvilkit export-plugin contract — security-hardened against hostile input by a 24-test battery in CI.

## Installation
lights up the "Download HTML" header action: clicking it runs the export end-to-end and emits
if the host wants to handle export itself — the action emits

instead.

## Core features

- **Standalone HTML5 output** — wrapped document with emitted

, and document title.
- **Built-in component emitters** — first-party HTML output for
are base64-inlined; larger or remote-only assets stay as URLs. Host controls the network via

enforced by a 24-test hostile-input battery, codified by the Anvilkit plugin trust model.
- **Direct-format access** —

is exported for headless export pipelines that don't run inside Studio.

Header action metadata:
— fired by the header action when
`{ formatId: "html", content: string, filename: string, mimeType: "text/html", warnings: ExportWarning[] }`
. Hosts handle this by running the export with their own IR + dispatching

(or by saving the result directly).

## Notes & FAQ

### Asset inlining is host-controlled

The plugin never fetches the network on its own — assets only inline when the host supplies a
. This keeps the export pipeline safe for sandboxed and offline environments. The pipeline order is

(replace asset IDs with data URLs).

### Built-in emitter coverage

The package ships first-party HTML emitters for nine component types:
. Components outside this set emit a warning and a stub

if you need full custom-component fidelity.

### When to opt out of
only when the host has wired an

listener — otherwise the user clicks "Download HTML" and nothing happens.

### Escaping contract

The escape contract is enforced by
(24-test hostile-input battery). Custom emitters that bypass

are CI-blocked.

### Alpha contract caveats

The emitted HTML/CSS string is not yet a stability contract. Class names, attribute ordering, and the embedded

block may change between alpha versions. Consumers that diff exported output across releases should snapshot only the rendered page, not the raw HTML.

### Bundle budget

— large IRs run linearly in the number of nodes.
